title: L1: Across Time and Space
description: Understanding that information can be shown in different ways depending on the task.

steps.title: Representations
steps.content:
Across time and space

A representation is a way of showing information. The same information can be represented in more than one way. The best representation depends on what the information is being used for.

LetterPurposeMeaning
AKeep informationSo it can be used later.
BSend informationSo another person or system can receive it.
CUse informationTo work something out or make a decision.
TaskHelpful representationWhy it helps
Find a roomMap or written directionsIt shows where to go.
Keep a list of scoresTableIt keeps names and numbers organised.
Warn someone quicklySign, light or soundIt can be noticed quickly.

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr1-intro-check',questions:[{"prompt":"A = information, B = representation. A map is a way of showing information. Type A or B.","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct: the map is the representation."},{"prompt":"A = information, B = representation. The fact Room 12 at 10:30 is being shown. Type A or B.","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct: that is the information."}]});

steps.title: Key Ideas: Information and Representation
steps.content:
Key ideas

Before answering questions, pupils need three clear ideas: information is the thing we want to show, a representation is the form we use to show it, and the task decides which form is most helpful.

WordSimple meaningExample
InformationThe thing we want to showThe room is Room 12
RepresentationThe form used to show itA map, a note, a table or a sound
TaskWhat we need to do with itKeep it, send it, or use it to decide something

In this lesson, do not try to find one perfect representation. Always ask: what is the task?

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr1-vocab-check',questions:[{"prompt":"A = information, B = representation, C = task. Deciding which train to catch is which one?","answers":["C","c"],"right":"Correct: deciding is the task."},{"prompt":"A = information, B = representation, C = task. A table used to show scores is which one?","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct: the table is a representation."}]});

steps.title: Step Through: Same Message
steps.content:
Step through a representation choice

Click through one message. Notice that we do not ask whether a representation is always best. We ask whether it fits the task.

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js:
BinaryLesson.createStepper({completeOnEnd:false,containerId:'y8dr1-stepper-1',steps:[{"title":"Start with the information","html":"

Room 12 at 10:30

","note":"This is the information before we choose a representation."},{"title":"Task 1: send the information now","html":"
Useful representationWhy
Spoken sentenceGood if the person is nearby.
Text messageGood if the person is not nearby.
","note":"For sending information, choose a representation that another person can receive and understand."},{"title":"Task 2: keep the information for tomorrow","html":"
Useful representationWhy
Calendar entryIt keeps the room and time together.
Written noteIt can be checked later.
","note":"For keeping information, choose a representation that can be saved and found again."},{"title":"Task 3: work out whether you are free","html":"
Useful representationWhy
Calendar dataA computer can compare it with other events.
TimetableYou can check whether the time clashes.
","note":"For using information, choose a representation that helps you work something out."}]});BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr1-guided1-check',questions:[{"prompt":"A = send now, B = keep for later, C = use to decide. A calendar entry for tomorrow fits which task?","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct: it keeps information for later."},{"prompt":"A = send now, B = keep for later, C = use to decide. A text message sent to a friend fits which task?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct: it sends information now."}]});

steps.title: Step Through: Pick the Best Form
steps.content:
Step through another example

This example uses the same information in three different ways.

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js:
BinaryLesson.createStepper({completeOnEnd:false,containerId:'y8dr1-stepper-2',steps:[{"title":"The information","html":"

A pupil scored 8 out of 10

","note":"The information is the score."},{"title":"If the task is to keep many scores","html":"
Best formReason
TableIt keeps names and scores lined up.
","note":"A table is useful when there are lots of similar records."},{"title":"If the task is to show progress","html":"
Best formReason
GraphIt makes change over time easier to see.
","note":"A graph is useful when the shape or pattern matters."},{"title":"If the task is to tell one pupil their mark","html":"
Best formReason
Spoken words or a written commentIt is direct and easy to understand.
","note":"The best representation changes when the task changes."}]});BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr1-guided2-check',questions:[{"prompt":"A = table, B = graph, C = alarm. Which is best for showing progress over several weeks?","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct: a graph shows change over time."},{"prompt":"A = table, B = graph, C = alarm. Which is best for keeping names and scores organised?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct: a table keeps records organised."}]});

steps.title: Mini Check: Identify the Parts
steps.content:
Mini check: identify the parts

These questions are deliberately small. Work out whether the question is asking about information, representation, or task.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr1-mini-check',questions:[{"prompt":"A question says: The temperature is 18 degrees. Is this information, a representation, or a task?","answers":["information"],"right":"Correct: 18 degrees is the information."},{"prompt":"A question says: The temperature is shown on a line graph. Is the line graph information, a representation, or a task?","answers":["representation","a representation"],"right":"Correct: the line graph is the representation."},{"prompt":"A question says: Decide whether the classroom is warm enough. Is this information, a representation, or a task?","answers":["task","a task"],"right":"Correct: deciding is the task."},{"prompt":"A = map, B = table, C = graph. Which is best for comparing scores over time?","answers":["C","c"],"right":"Correct: a graph helps show change."},{"prompt":"A = map, B = table, C = graph. Which is best for keeping names and scores organised?","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct: a table keeps records organised."}]});

steps.title: Checked Practice 1
steps.content:
Checked practice: match the task

Use the table: A = keep information, B = send information, C = use information to work something out. Type A, B or C.

LetterPurposeMeaning
AKeep informationSo it can be used later.
BSend informationSo another person or system can receive it.
CUse informationTo work something out or make a decision.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr1-practice-a',questions:[{"prompt":"A pupil saves a homework deadline in a calendar so they can check it tomorrow.","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct: the information is being kept for later."},{"prompt":"A fire alarm makes a loud sound so people know to leave.","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct: the information is being sent quickly."},{"prompt":"A spreadsheet adds marks and calculates a total.","answers":["C","c"],"right":"Correct: the information is being used to work something out."},{"prompt":"A teacher emails a room change to the class.","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct: the information is being sent."},{"prompt":"A website sorts prices from lowest to highest.","answers":["C","c"],"right":"Correct: sorting is using information to produce a result."}]});

steps.title: Checked Practice 2
steps.content:
Checked practice: choose from clear options

Type A, B or C for the most suitable representation.

LetterRepresentation
AMap or directions
BTable
CWarning sign or alarm
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr1-practice-b',questions:[{"prompt":"Which representation would help someone find a classroom?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct: a map or directions show where to go."},{"prompt":"Which representation would keep pupil names and scores organised?","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct: a table keeps rows and columns organised."},{"prompt":"Which representation would quickly tell people there is danger?","answers":["C","c"],"right":"Correct: a warning sign or alarm is quick to notice."},{"prompt":"Which representation would help compare several test results?","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct: a table makes comparison easier."},{"prompt":"Which representation would be best for showing a route?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct: a map or directions show a route."}]});

steps.title: Extension: Explain Your Choice
steps.content:
Extension challenge

Fast finishers should practise explaining why a representation fits the task. Choose the best option, then read the feedback carefully.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr1-extension',questions:[{"prompt":"A = graph, B = alarm sound, C = table. Which is best for warning people immediately?","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct: an alarm sound can be noticed straight away."},{"prompt":"A = table, B = map, C = spoken sentence. Which is best for keeping 30 pupils and scores organised?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct: a table handles repeated records clearly."},{"prompt":"A = map, B = paragraph, C = random number. Which is best for showing where the library is?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct: a map shows location."},{"prompt":"A = graph, B = single word, C = alarm. Which is best for seeing whether attendance is improving over weeks?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct: a graph shows change over time."}]});

steps.title: Endless Practice
steps.content:
Endless practice

Answer 5 simple representation questions correctly to complete this step. Each question tells you the options to choose from.
steps.js: (function(){var bank=[{"prompt":"A = keep, B = send, C = use. A timetable is checked to decide which train to catch. Type A, B or C.","answers":["C","c"],"right":"Correct: the information is used to make a decision."},{"prompt":"A = keep, B = send, C = use. A message is sent to tell someone the room number. Type A, B or C.","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct: the information is sent."},{"prompt":"A = keep, B = send, C = use. A file is saved so it can be opened next lesson. Type A, B or C.","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct: the information is kept."},{"prompt":"A = map, B = table, C = warning sign. Which helps someone follow a route?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = map, B = table, C = warning sign. Which keeps scores in rows and columns?","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct."}];var qs=[];for(var i=0;i<5;i++){qs.push(bank[Math.floor(Math.random()*bank.length)]);}BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr1-endless',questions:qs});})();

steps.title: End of Lesson Exam
steps.content:
End of lesson exam

Use what you practised: identify the task, then choose the matching purpose or representation.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr1-exam',questions:[{"prompt":"What is a representation?","answers":["a way of showing information","way of showing information","showing information"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = keep, B = send, C = use. A computer compares two dates to find the earlier one. Type A, B or C.","answers":["C","c"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = map, B = table, C = warning sound. Which is best for showing a route?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = map, B = table, C = warning sound. Which is best for organised names and scores?","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = keep, B = send, C = use. A note is saved for tomorrow. Type A, B or C.","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."}]});

quizQuestions.q: Which is best for showing a route through a building?
A file is saved so it can be opened tomorrow. What is the task?
A warning alarm is used so people know to leave. What is the task?
A spreadsheet calculates a total from marks. What is the task?
options: Use information to work something out

quizQuestions.q: Which representation is useful for names and scores in rows and columns?
Why might the same information be represented in different ways?
options: Only one representation can ever work
